title: Understanding Centripetal Acceleration
text: Centripetal acceleration is the acceleration that keeps an object moving in a circular path. It is directed towards the center of the circle.

title: Formula for Centripetal Acceleration
text: The formula for centripetal acceleration is given by $$a_c=\frac{v^2}{r}$$, where v is the velocity and $$r$$ is the radius of the circular path.

title: Using the Banking Angle
text: In an ideally banked turn, the centripetal force is provided by the horizontal component of the normal force. The angle of banking helps determine the radius of the turn.

title: Calculate the Radius of the Turn
text: Use the formula $$tan(theta)=\frac{v^2}{r g}$$ to find the radius $$r$$, where theta is the banking angle, v is the velocity, and g is the acceleration due to gravity $$(9.8$$ m/s**2).

title: Calculate the Centripetal Acceleration
text: Now that you have the radius, use the formula $$a_c=\frac{v^2}{r}$$ to find the centripetal acceleration.
